tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58018,"title":"[PostgreSQL] Docs: README examples are incomplete — missing Build() and query usage","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` **Service directory:** `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58012) by `@mattboentoro` ## What Changed The `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` 1.0.0-beta.1 release preparation commit was pushed, marking the first public beta of this library. It provides Entra ID (AAD) passwordless authentication for the Npgsql PostgreSQL driver via `UseEntraAuthentication` / `UseEntraAuthenticationAsync` extension methods on `NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder`. ## Gaps Found - **Incomplete examples in README**: Both the "Synchronous authentication" and "Asynchronous authentication" sections end at `builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential)` without calling `builder.Build()` — users are left without the step needed to actually create a usable `NpgsqlDataSource` - **No end-to-end query example in README**: `samples/Sample01_HelloWorld.md` shows the full flow (`Build()` → `OpenConnectionAsync()` → `ExecuteScalarAsync()`), but the main README `Examples` section never demonstrates how to execute a query against the database - **Explicit username pattern missing from README**: The `Key concepts` section explains username extraction but provides no code example for the explicit-username override pattern (passing `Username=my-db-role` in the connection string), which is a common advanced use case - **Redundant "Authenticate the client" and "Synchronous authentication" snippets**: `Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Authenticate` and `Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync` (backed by `ReadMeSnippets.Authenticate()` and `ReadMeSnippets.SynchronousAuthentication()`) render identical code in the README, wasting space and confusing readers 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/README.md`** 1. **Collapse the "Authenticate the client" snippet** — replace the inline code block with prose pointing readers to the Examples section (removing the duplicate `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Authenticate` placeholder), OR merge it with the sync example below. 2. **Extend the "Synchronous authentication" example** to include `builder.Build()`: Replace: ```` ``` with a snippet that also calls `builder.Build()` (update the region in the test file accordingly — see Step 2). 3. **Extend the "Asynchronous authentication" example** similarly to include `builder.Build()`. 4. **Add a "Query the database" subsection** under `## Examples` with a new snippet placeholder: ```markdown ### Query the database Once the data source is built, open a connection and execute queries as usual: ```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query ``` ``` 5. **Add an "Explicit database role name" subsection** under `## Examples`: ```markdown ### Explicit database role name If the PostgreSQL role name differs from the Entra ID identity name, include `Username` in the connection string. The library will skip token-based username extraction and use the provided value: ```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **File: `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/tests/Samples/ReadMeSnippets.cs`** 1. Update `SynchronousAuthentication()` to include `Build()` inside the region: ```csharp [Test] public async Task SynchronousAuthentication() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=localhost;Database=testdb"); #endif builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #endregion } ``` 2. Update `AsynchronousAuthentication()` similarly: ```csharp [Test] public async Task AsynchronousAuthentication() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Async #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=localhost;Database=testdb"); #endif await builder.UseEntraAuthenticationAsync(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #endregion } ``` 3. Add new `ReadMeQuery()` method for the query snippet (wrapped in `#if SNIPPET` because it requires a live database): ```csharp [Test] [Category("Live")] public async Task ReadMeQuery() { #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query await using var connection = await dataSource.OpenConnectionAsync(); await using var cmd = new NpgsqlCommand("SELECT version()", connection); var version = await cmd.ExecuteScalarAsync(); #endregion #else await Task.CompletedTask; #endif } ``` 4. Add new `ReadMeExplicitUsername()` method: ```csharp [Test] public void ReadMeExplicitUsername() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var connectionString = "Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;Username=my-db-role;SSL Mode=Require"; #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var connectionString = "Host=localhost;Database=testdb;Username=my-db-role"; #endif var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder(connectionString); builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); #endregion } ``` Snippet name mapping: | Snippet name | Test method | README section | |---|---|---| | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync` | `SynchronousAuthentication` | Examples → Synchronous authentication | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Async` | `AsynchronousAuthentication` | Examples → Asynchronous authentication | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query` | `ReadMeQuery` | Examples → Query the database | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername` | `ReadMeExplicitUsername` | Examples → Explicit database role name | ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/README.md` already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). No sections need to be added, only the Examples section content needs to be extended as described above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 postgresql` — verify the README is updated with the new snippet content by reviewing `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 postgresql` — no public API changes expected; run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above ```` > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24207149224/agentic_workflow) · ● 529.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58018","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T18:46:44Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T18:46:44Z"}

{"number":58018,"title":"[PostgreSQL] Docs: README examples are incomplete — missing Build() and query usage","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` **Service directory:** `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58012) by `@mattboentoro` ## What Changed The `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` 1.0.0-beta.1 release preparation commit was pushed, marking the first public beta of this library. It provides Entra ID (AAD) passwordless authentication for the Npgsql PostgreSQL driver via `UseEntraAuthentication` / `UseEntraAuthenticationAsync` extension methods on `NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder`. ## Gaps Found - **Incomplete examples in README**: Both the "Synchronous authentication" and "Asynchronous authentication" sections end at `builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential)` without calling `builder.Build()` — users are left without the step needed to actually create a usable `NpgsqlDataSource` - **No end-to-end query example in README**: `samples/Sample01_HelloWorld.md` shows the full flow (`Build()` → `OpenConnectionAsync()` → `ExecuteScalarAsync()`), but the main README `Examples` section never demonstrates how to execute a query against the database - **Explicit username pattern missing from README**: The `Key concepts` section explains username extraction but provides no code example for the explicit-username override pattern (passing `Username=my-db-role` in the connection string), which is a common advanced use case - **Redundant "Authenticate the client" and "Synchronous authentication" snippets**: `Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Authenticate` and `Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync` (backed by `ReadMeSnippets.Authenticate()` and `ReadMeSnippets.SynchronousAuthentication()`) render identical code in the README, wasting space and confusing readers 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/README.md`** 1. **Collapse the "Authenticate the client" snippet** — replace the inline code block with prose pointing readers to the Examples section (removing the duplicate `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Authenticate` placeholder), OR merge it with the sync example below. 2. **Extend the "Synchronous authentication" example** to include `builder.Build()`: Replace: ```` ``` with a snippet that also calls `builder.Build()` (update the region in the test file accordingly — see Step 2). 3. **Extend the "Asynchronous authentication" example** similarly to include `builder.Build()`. 4. **Add a "Query the database" subsection** under `## Examples` with a new snippet placeholder: ```markdown ### Query the database Once the data source is built, open a connection and execute queries as usual: ```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query ``` ``` 5. **Add an "Explicit database role name" subsection** under `## Examples`: ```markdown ### Explicit database role name If the PostgreSQL role name differs from the Entra ID identity name, include `Username` in the connection string. The library will skip token-based username extraction and use the provided value: ```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **File: `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/tests/Samples/ReadMeSnippets.cs`** 1. Update `SynchronousAuthentication()` to include `Build()` inside the region: ```csharp [Test] public async Task SynchronousAuthentication() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=localhost;Database=testdb"); #endif builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #endregion } ``` 2. Update `AsynchronousAuthentication()` similarly: ```csharp [Test] public async Task AsynchronousAuthentication() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Async #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=localhost;Database=testdb"); #endif await builder.UseEntraAuthenticationAsync(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #endregion } ``` 3. Add new `ReadMeQuery()` method for the query snippet (wrapped in `#if SNIPPET` because it requires a live database): ```csharp [Test] [Category("Live")] public async Task ReadMeQuery() { #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query await using var connection = await dataSource.OpenConnectionAsync(); await using var cmd = new NpgsqlCommand("SELECT version()", connection); var version = await cmd.ExecuteScalarAsync(); #endregion #else await Task.CompletedTask; #endif } ``` 4. Add new `ReadMeExplicitUsername()` method: ```csharp [Test] public void ReadMeExplicitUsername() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var connectionString = "Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;Username=my-db-role;SSL Mode=Require"; #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var connectionString = "Host=localhost;Database=testdb;Username=my-db-role"; #endif var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder(connectionString); builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); #endregion } ``` Snippet name mapping: | Snippet name | Test method | README section | |---|---|---| | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync` | `SynchronousAuthentication` | Examples → Synchronous authentication | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Async` | `AsynchronousAuthentication` | Examples → Asynchronous authentication | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query` | `ReadMeQuery` | Examples → Query the database | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername` | `ReadMeExplicitUsername` | Examples → Explicit database role name | ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/README.md` already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). No sections need to be added, only the Examples section content needs to be extended as described above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 postgresql` — verify the README is updated with the new snippet content by reviewing `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 postgresql` — no public API changes expected; run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above ```` > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24207149224/agentic_workflow) · ● 529.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58018","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T18:46:44Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T18:46:44Z"}

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in the `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` 1.0.0-beta.1 README, specifically missing `Build()` calls in authentication examples, no end-to-end query example, no explicit-username example, and redundant code snippets. It includes a detailed implementation guide for resolving the documentation gaps.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` 1.0.0-beta.1  
**Affected API:** `NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder.UseEntraAuthentication()` / `UseEntraAuthenticationAsync()`  
**Scenarios:**
- Synchronous and async authentication examples in README omit the required `builder.Build()` call
- No end-to-end query example demonstrating how to use the built `NpgsqlDataSource`
- Explicit database role name override pattern (passing `Username=my-db-role` in connection string) has no code example
- Duplicate code rendered by `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Authenticate` and `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync` snippets

**Root ask:** Update README examples and test snippet methods to be complete and non-redundant, covering the full authentication-to-query flow and the explicit username pattern.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is an automated doc-gap report triggered by commit `[REDACTED]` (PR #58012). No runtime failure or exception — purely a documentation completeness issue.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/README.md` — confirm the synchronous and async examples end before `Build()`, and that the `Authenticate the client` and `Synchronous authentication` snippets render identical code
2. Review `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/tests/Samples/ReadMeSnippets.cs` — verify the current snippet regions and add/extend methods as described in the implementation guide
